item.title: NDIS cuts could create unsafe environments and domestic violence risks, inquiry warned
item.description: Planned cuts to NDIS community participation budgets could leave participants in unsafe environments, including increasing their risk of domestic violence, advocates have warned.

item.title: Bear tranquilised after roaming suburb in Japan
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Japan's bear attacks are breaking records and spilling into big cities
item.description: Bear encounters are rising in Japan, and the problem is not confined to the wilderness.

item.title: Traveller dies after contracting Murray Valley Encephalitis in WA
item.description: One person has died after contracting Murray Valley Encephalitis in Western Australia's Kimberley region.

item.title: José Carreras to play Gabba show with Robbie Williams
item.description: Revered opera star José Carreras will lead a line-up featuring everyone from Robbie Williams to Sheppard at the Gabba's first concert in eight years.

item.title: Inpex seeks urgent orders to stop Darwin strike as experts fear impact on gas market could be significant
item.description: Oil and gas giant Inpex has launched proceedings with the Fair Work Commission seeking urgent orders to stop strike action at its Ichthys LNG facility in Darwin.

item.title: Beekeepers call for biopesticide known to target varroa mite to come to Australia
item.description: A new biopesticide could help battle growing resistance to varroa mite treatments in Australia.

item.title: Traditional owners' $2 million loss as Nathan River Resources collapses 'undermines' promises
item.description: Nathan River Resources went into administration and owes about $360 million to creditors including the NT government and traditional owners.

item.title: Mark Schwarzer backs Socceroos to make FIFA World Cup impression in US
item.description: Maligned, underestimated and overlooked — and that is just the way we like it according to the Socceroos' most capped player.

item.title: Skipper recounts mission to rescue dog swept out to sea
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Military officers behind acid attack on Indonesian activist sentenced to jail
item.description: Four military intelligence officers are sentenced to up to three years in jail, after they threw sulphuric acid over a human rights activist in a brazen attack in Central Jakarta.

item.title: TGA cracks down on unregulated peptides, says increased imports are posing a risk to consumer safety
item.description: The Therapeutic Goods Administration has launched a crackdown on unregulated peptides, saying increased illegal imports were posing a risk to consumers.

item.title: Canberrans looking for cost-of-living relief in ACT budget might be left wanting
item.description: During practice at this Canberra football club, players discuss the ways they are saving money. But if they were looking for cost-of-living relief in today's ACT budget, they might not have found what they were looking for.

item.title: Media outlets ask Supreme Court judge to overturn magistrate’s suppression order in Cairns extortion case
item.description: A non-publication order suppressing the identity of a high-profile Queensland man named in a Cairns extortion case will be tested in the Supreme Court.

item.title: Former NT Minister claims child neglect is being 'hushed up' in Northern Territory Indigenous communities
item.description: A review has begun into the family circumstances of Kumanjayi Little Baby leading up to her disappearance and death, which relatives hope will find answers.

item.title: Saltwater crocodile injures man at Barred Creek, north of Broome
item.description: Authorities have confirmed the 35-year-old man was fishing in waist deep water at Barred Creek, north of Broome, when he stood on the crocodile.

item.title: Seven people and two companies charged after deadly Hong Kong fire
item.description: The seven people charged included directors and inspectors of a consultancy firm involved in the estate's renovation.

item.title: Dog swept out to sea rescued by tour boat off English coast
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Dog rescued off English coast after being blown out to sea in inflatable kayak
item.description: Bruce, a dog, has been rescued from the North Sea after drifting away in an inflatable kayak.

item.title: Coroner releases findings into death of Rosemarie Campbell after gastric bypass
item.description: A woman who died days after gastric bypass surgery likely would have survived if she had not been discharged, an inquest has found.

item.title: Belfast stabbing suspect Hadi Alodid in court as victim Stephen Ogilvie loses eye
item.description: Hadi Alodid, a 30-year-old man from Sudan, appears in a Belfast court charged with attempted murder over a stabbing attack that triggered anti-immigrant violence in several parts of Northern Ireland.

item.title: England drops Stokes from second NZ Test amid 'ongoing investigation'
item.description: The decision to leave both the English captain and bowler Gus Atkinson off the side comes after a reported nightclub incident following the side's win in the first Test.

item.title: In Moldova, I meet a woman with a dangerous plan to save her life
item.description: On a park bench in Moldova, I meet a Ukrainian refugee who is planning to return home through a war zone for cancer treatment. We talk for hours.

item.title: Steven Spielberg returns to sci-fi and alien wonder in his thrilling Disclosure Day
item.description: ET director Steven Spielberg dives back into the extra terrestrial with his latest film while asking 'How do you astonish an audience when anything is possible, and nothing is real?

item.title: Quiz: Do you know where these famous Australian books are set?
item.description: Occupy yourself with some armchair travel courtesy of a selection of our nation's most esteemed literary works.

item.title: How the Belfast attack played into the hands of Britain's far right
item.description: As UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer fights for his political life, the country is bracing for what could be a yet another summer of unrest.

item.title: Albanese's frustration with Trump is on display as he pays a price for an unpopular war
item.description: The Albanese government is paying an economic and political toll for an ongoing and deeply unpopular war.

item.title: Solariums operating underground despite decade-long commercial ban
item.description: Illegal solariums are operating across the country, from single machines secretly set up in suburban neighbourhood garages to those hidden in the back of legitimate businesses.

item.title: Victorian town fights against high suicide rates with community-led prevention training
item.description: After losing a family member to suicide, Peter Taylor vowed to bring mental health first aid to his community.

item.title: Tasmanian Senator Wendy Askew retires with fellow Liberals, One Nation eyeing seat
item.description: A Tasmanian senator will not recontest her seat in 2028, meaning it is up for the taking by Liberal colleagues and possibly a resurgent One Nation.

item.title: World's largest whale graveyard found in the Indian Ocean
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Residents of new outer-city developments make plea for better public transport in south-east Queensland
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of new homes are being built on the outskirts of greater Brisbane. But residents are concerned if one thing doesn't improve, their quality of life will go backwards.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: A peculiar peace prize, sky-high tickets and threatened boycotts
item.description: Struggled to keep up with the rollercoaster of controversies that plagued the build-up to this World Cup? We've got you covered.

item.title: Extra maternal and child health nurse visits for children in Victoria under Thriving Kids program
item.description: All children in Victoria will get two extra visits with maternal and child health nurses as the state prepares to launch its new program for those shifted off the NDIS.

item.title: Road trauma victims call for 'overhaul' of Transport Accident Commission in parliamentary inquiry
item.description: A road accident survivor says the organisation that helped her defy all expectations has become a nightmare to deal with.

item.title: The ACT budget quantifies 'the Trump effect' at $2.3m a day. But homegrown decisions are more costly
item.description: The US President, arguably more than the territory's own Treasurer Chris Steel, is very directly adding to the heavy weight dragging on public finances in the nation's capital. Here's how.

item.title: Petrol station moguls pay $2 million to creditors after empire collapses, land contaminated
item.description: The directors of Zoya Investments have agreed to a $2 million settlement to end the liquidator's case against them.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Mexico City welcomes World Cup but reality bites
item.description: As Mexicans prepare to host or co-host the FIFA World Cup for a record third time, many locals are frustrated by being locked out of the tournament, while others are more optimistic.

item.title: Sydney psychiatrist Gopi Ilawala allowed to continue practising after sexual assault conviction
item.description: The psychiatrist sexually assaulted a colleague in 2022 but a tribunal has "reprimanded" him and allowed him to continue working in medicine.

item.title: Australia records six-wicket win over West Indies in final Women's T20 World Cup warm-up match
item.description: Australia goes into the Women's T20 World Cup in a positive frame of mind, after Georgia Voll leads a dominant win over West Indies in their final warm-up match in Cardiff.

item.title: Missing Nemo from tropical island highlights threat of illegal poaching to Great Barrier Reef
item.description: The little clownfish known as "Island Nemo" was always there for locals and tourists snorkelling in a tropical bay. But then it disappeared and locals suspect foul play.

item.title: Dark Mofo festival begins today in Hobart, including iconic Winter Feast banquet
item.description: Tasmania's two-week Dark Mofo festival kicks off today in Hobart. Popular events such as Winter Feast, Night Mass, the Nude Solstice Swim and the Ogoh Ogoh procession return, while one of the new Spirit of Tasmania ferries will host a free art exhibition.

item.title: Socceroos prepare for the FIFA World Cup
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Will Elon Musk become the world’s first trillionaire?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Grandfather's close call inspires Sunshine Coast student's smart clock invention
item.description: After it took nearly two days for his grandfather to be found following a serious fall, Kai Gould has created a device to connect older people living independently with their loved ones.

item.title: Warning oil could surge to $US150 a barrel with inventories 'critically low' — as it happened
item.description: A major Australian bank has warned the price of oil is at risk of surging to $US150 per barrel as oil inventories approach critically low levels.

item.title: Towns along 400km of WA coastline battle extreme erosion from winter storms
item.description: A week after Western Australia experienced one of its worst storms in decades, some stretches of coast are crumbling into the ocean at an alarming rate. Millions of dollars' worth of infrastructure is at risk heading into winter.

item.title: Donald Trump says US secretly helped ships move oil through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The US president says his military has been running a "secret mission" to help tankers and commercial ships defy Iran's blockade.

item.title: Scientists discover 149 new marine species off Christmas and Cocos (Keeling) Islands
item.description: Researchers have catalogued dozens of new marine species found in the depths of Australia's most remote waters. The result is a window into a rarely glimpsed world.

item.title: Opposition leader Angus Taylor rules out carving up seats with One Nation as party raises $2m
item.description: Opposition leader Angus Taylor says the Coalition has no plan to share the seats the party targets at the next federal election with One Nation as Pauline Hanson's party says it has raised $2 million since launch.

item.title: The $12 Billion World Cup: Inside FIFA’s Biggest Gamble
item.description: Sport news, live scores, features, analysis and photos from the world of sport. Comprehensive coverage of AFL, cricket, football, rugby league and rugby union. Audio interviews. News stories and highlights in video. Sports news bulletin.

item.title: South Australia secures AFL Gather Round hosting rights until 2029
item.description: The AFL's Gather Round will stay in South Australia until 2029 under a new three-year partnership. It will include a parade and a "feature match" but no decision has been made on venues.

item.title: Solomon Islands won't release security pact with China because of non-disclosure clause
item.description: Prime Minister Matthew Wale says it is legally impossible to publish the final document because the Sogavare government made binding commitments to the Chinese government when it signed the agreement in 2022.

item.title: Gout Gout would be wise to take heed of Letsile Tebogo's advice after Diamond League debut
item.description: Gout Gout can benefit from Letsile Tebogo's wisdom, as the Olympic champion knows what it is like to make the challenging transition from junior star to the senior ranks.

item.title: Culture in Australia's largest police force 'unacceptable'
item.description: Bullying, discrimination and sexual harassment are occurring at "unacceptable levels" within the NSW Police Force and employees are too afraid to speak up, an independent report has found.

item.title: Albany musician Eddy Jay turns to 3D printer for new supply of musical instruments
item.description: After a music career that's taken him around the world, Eddy Jay has turned to cutting-edge manufacturing to make his own instruments.

item.title: Queensland hospice's end-of-life service kept alive after 'generous' donations
item.description: A regional Queensland palliative care facility has been given a new lease on life after receiving two major donations from businesses in Hervey Bay.

item.title: Launceston City Council receives $100k in insurance after trophy, featuring 24-carat gold sheep, stolen
item.description: The trophy features a sculpture that includes a 24-carat gold merino sheep and a weaving loom made of silver and platinum, with its theft last year captured on CCTV.

item.title: SA consumer watchdog says real estate agents can be liable if aware of property issues
item.description: Real estate agents selling homes must not misrepresent information about the builder or serious defects, SA's consumer watchdog has said, after eight builders were hit with public warning notices over their conduct in the state.

item.title: Confirmed Ebola virus cases near 600 as outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo grows
item.description: In just two weeks the number of confirmed Ebola virus cases has increased more than eightfold, with at least 100 people now dead.

item.title: World Cup: Socceroos star Mo Toure misses training days out from opening game against Türkiye
item.description: Socceroos striker Mo Toure is a no-show at the beginning of a scheduled training session, bringing his fitness into doubt just days out from their World Cup opener against Türkiye.

item.title: Girlfriend of man shot by police disputes that officers told him to drop knife
item.description: A coronial inquest into the shooting has heard from the woman who was in a locked bathroom with her armed partner for hours of police negotiations.

item.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup kick-off times in Australian time, when to watch in Australia
item.description: Check out when your team's matches are kicking off at the 2026 FIFA World Cup so you don't miss a match.

item.title: State and territory disability ministers lash federal government's NDIS changes
item.description: State and territory disability ministers have accused the Albanese government of pushing on with NDIS reform that does not align with previous agreements.

item.title: World Cup: FIFA chief Gianni Infantino dismisses concerns over ticketing, visa issues
item.description: FIFA president Gianni Infantino defends soccer's governing body's handling of visa issues and ticket prices ahead of the World Cup.

item.title: Coronial probe into Melbourne aged care COVID outbreak resumes after years-long hiatus
item.description: An inquest examining 50 COVID-era deaths at a Melbourne aged care home has resumed after a years-long hiatus.

item.title: Man accused of forging estranged wife's death certificate prohibited from selling shared properties
item.description: The estranged husband of Rehana Parvin, whose body was found in Bangladesh in 2024, will not be allowed to sell the three homes they owned together.

item.title: Adelaide men jailed for $6 million fraudulent property investment scheme
item.description: Two men who "exploited the vulnerability" of multiple victims by swindling millions of dollars in a fraudulent property investment scheme have been jailed for the "undoubtedly serious" offending.

item.title: Casey McLean's arrival to State of Origin is proof the future is now
item.description: By the first month of his 20s, Casey McLean had already achieved more than most players do in a lifetime but ahead of his first run-on Origin match the first of a new generation of stars is still just getting started.

item.title: Former police officer who harassed woman online sentenced to six months' prison
item.description: A former Victoria Police officer is sentenced to six months in prison after repeatedly harassing a woman on social media.

item.title: Daylesford pub did not hold valid outdoor dining permit at time of deadly crash, court hears
item.description: A Victorian court hears that a regional pub where five people died when a driver had a hypoglycaemic episode, crashing his car through diners, did not have a valid outdoor dining permit at the time of the crash.

item.title: Anti-immigration figures seizing on outrage at Northern Ireland stabbing to drive people towards cause
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk and far-right activist Tommy Robinson have seized upon the alleged stabbing of a man in Belfast by a Sudanese immigrant to push an anti-immigration agenda.

item.title: RecipeTin Eats founder Nagi adopts new canine companion
item.description: Nagi Maehashi, the founder of RecipeTin Eats, introduces her new canine companion Jaffle.

item.title: FIFA president on Iran's participation in 2026 World Cup
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Alleged arsonist Jonathan Rinderknecht lonely, dejected when he lit Palisades fire, court hears
item.description: Jonathan Rinderknecht's attorney countered that a hilltop blaze was triggered by fireworks, not his client, and that the massive Palisades inferno that killed 12 people was a separate fire set by others.

item.title: Dictor Dongrin's death in custody subject of critical findings by NSW coroner
item.description: A NSW coroner recommends conduct reviews for medical staff over their treatment of a prisoner who died within a day of entering Australia's largest prison.

item.title: Andrew Mungatopi sentenced to 10 years in jail for stabbing death of Tiwi Islands sistergirl
item.description: Andrew Mungatopi has been sentenced to 10 years and two months in prison for the fatal stabbing of his niece in a Darwin backyard in 2024.

item.title: Emotional Hanson blames Abbott for electoral fraud 'witch hunt'
item.description: As Anthony Albanese stood in front of a Medicare banner and Pauline Hanson put her hand out for donations, voters could be forgiven for thinking the election was two months, not two years, away.

item.title: Adelaide lawyer Andrew Graham pleads not guilty to sexual offences
item.description: Andrew Graham enters not guilty pleas to two counts of rape and one charge of compelling a person to sexually manipulate an offender.

item.title: BHP facing 'historic' strike as union members back industrial action in Port Hedland
item.description: Unionised workers at BHP's operations in Port Hedland could walk off the job as soon as next week after endorsing protected industrial action.

item.title: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson says she takes policy advice from 'friend' Gina Rinehart
item.description: Pauline Hanson speaks of her "respect" for billionaire Gina Rinehart , becoming emotional during a speech recalling her time in detention.

item.title: 'Inspirational' Dawson stars as Crows send premiership statement
item.description: Jordan Dawson plays one of the best quarters of his career as Adelaide claims a 57-point win over the Western Bulldogs at Docklands.

item.title: 'Trying not to cry': Rabbitohs rise after emotional Jai Arrow moment
item.description: Jai Arrow has inspired South Sydney to a commanding win over the Brisbane Broncos, on an emotional night at Stadium Australia. Follow live.

item.title: Thai court sentences two Uyghur men to death for 2015 Bangkok bombing
item.description: The men were accused of carrying out the attack on the popular Erawan Shrine that killed 20 people, many of whom were Chinese tourists.

item.title: Thursday finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Rosemarie Campbell's family calls for accountability after coroner finds death was preventable
item.description: The family of a Gold Coast woman who died following complications from a surgery performed by Reza Adib have called for accountability and meaningful change in the wake of damning inquest findings.

item.title: Byron Bay Bluesfest liquidator’s report reveals creditors owed $7 million
item.description: A report into the cancelled Byron Bay Bluesfest reveals creditors are unlikely to be repaid the millions of dollars they are owed.

item.title: Gold Coast Suns' Max Knobel suspended for four AFL/VFL games over homophobic slur
item.description: Gold Coast Suns ruck Max Knobel is banned over a homophobic slur during a VFL game, meaning he will be unavailable until after round 17 of the AFL season.

item.title: Richard Marles 'stood up' on AUKUS trip after British counterpart John Healey's shock resignation
item.description: Defence Minister Richard Marles's visit to the United Kingdom has been thrown into chaos after his British counterpart John Healey resigned hours before they were due to speak.

item.title: Six legendary Australian music acts inducted into ARIA Hall of Fame after 'a pretty amazing journey'
item.description: Gurrumul, Jenny Morris, Kate Ceberano, Spiderbait, The Living End, and Vika and Linda Bull were elevated six to top-tier status at a ceremony in Sydney as part of ARIA's 40th anniversary celebrations.

item.title: US forecasters say potentially record-breaking El Niño underway
item.description: There is a high chance this year's Pacific climate phenomenon will be among the strongest on record, which could lead to hotter temperatures across the globe.

item.title: Iran signals caution as Trump says peace deal could come 'this weekend' — as it happened
item.description: Iran says a final decision has not yet been made on a possible peace agreement with the US, after Donald Trump claimed one could be signed this weekend. Read how events unfolded on Friday, June 12.

item.title: South Korea completes Czechia comeback, relief for Socceroos star
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup gets underway as South Korea comes from a goal down to beat Czechia 2-1 and Mexico defeats South Africa 2-0 in an opening game which features three red cards.

item.title: This man bought the land stolen from his Indigenous ancestors
item.description: Gunditjmara man Craig Molyneux buys back land that was stolen from his ancestors and infuses the property's old church with his Indigenous culture.

item.title: Abdul Abdullah reflects on fraught legacy of Cronulla riots in triptych for Sydney Biennale
item.description: Artist Abdul Abdullah says the Cronulla riots were a "moment of revelation" for many Australians as he unveils a new work for Sydney Biennale.

item.title: ABC news quiz: Fake weather, a musical snub, a whale graveyard and James Bond going woke
item.description: Don't let your confidence be shaken or stirred by this week's test of how much you've been paying attention.

item.title: International students in Australia face more financial pressure as currencies weaken in parts of Asia
item.description: When Jolie Cao chose to do her undergraduate university degree in Australia, she knew it would be expensive. But an additional financial pressure has made things "really hard".

item.title: How you and your superannuation came to be aboard SpaceX
item.description: If your superannuation is managed by any of Australia's big superannuation funds, chances are you're about to become a highly valued financial backer to Elon Musk.

item.title: World Cup 2026: How Ronaldo and Messi are pushing football's age boundaries
item.description: A record number of players over 40 will feature at this year's World Cup. So what part does sports science play in keeping the likes of Cristiano Ronaldo on the pitch longer?

item.title: ABC More or Less quiz: World Cup edition
item.description: Which team has won the most World Cup matches? Has anyone lost more than England? Take our World Cup ranking quiz to find out.

item.title: How One Nation could disrupt Australia's progressive stronghold
item.description: The idea that One Nation could come close to the government benches was absurd six months ago, but it is a prospect Victorian Labor MPs and insiders are increasingly discussing.

item.title: Privacy commissioner gags complainant over American Express data security findings
item.description: Australia's privacy commissioner has threatened a complainant with legal action to prevent the full disclosure of the findings of a long-running investigation into American Express's information security, which found widespread technology failures.

item.title: 'Broken promise' or not, Anthony Albanese's tax decision has helped One Nation
item.description: The prime minister's cavalier breaking of election promises has poured kerosene on the fire under a party that can no longer be called "minor".

item.title: Australia loses ODI series to Bangladesh after three ducks in the first two overs
item.description: Australia loses a series to Bangladesh for the first time after a disastrous start with the bat in Dhaka.

item.title: One year after Air India crash, victims' families wait for investigation report
item.description: One year on from the fatal Air India crash that claimed 260 lives, families of victims are still waiting for a report into the investigation.

item.title: Major PFAS class action could lead to multi-billion-dollar claim figure
item.description: Hundreds of businesses and organisations are part of a major Victorian Supreme Court class action against chemical manufacturer 3M.

item.title: Country swimmer trains in 25m local pool to swim 45km around New York
item.description: While training in a small NSW country town, cold-water swimmer Peta Bradley is preparing to tackle one of the world's most demanding open-water challenges, a 45-kilometre circumnavigation of Manhattan Island.

item.title: Brisbane Broncos still alive in NRL premiership race despite losing streak, says coach Michael Maguire
item.description: Brisbane coach Michael Maguire declares the Broncos are "still well and truly alive" in the wake of their 42-point belting at the hands of South Sydney on Thursday night.

item.title: Australian couples opt for micro-weddings and elopements amid cost-of-living crisis
item.description: Melbourne couple Averill and Lloyd Johnson pulled off their dream wedding for just $3,000, compared to the average wedding cost of about $38,000.

item.title: Queensland beekeepers desperate for new varroa mite treatments as colonies collapse
item.description: With many managed colonies under threat from the invasive varroa mite, Queensland beekeepers are trying to find ways to protect their industry.

item.title: How blood donations are helping three-year-old with rare disorder play like a 'normal kid'
item.description: Lauren Zeller has a one-in-a-million bleeding disorder, but strangers' blood donations give her a chance to play like other kids.

item.title: Tasmanian Aboriginal community calls for change after damage to heritage site at Trevallyn Reserve
item.description: Upgrades to an existing walking and cycling path have built over what an Aboriginal organisation describes as a "significant" cultural site in northern Tasmania — and it didn't require a permit.

item.title: Man fined thousands for 'silly' illegal dumping of tyres at Moonta Mines national heritage site
item.description: A young man who could have paid $115 to discard a trailer load of tyres has been fined thousands for illegally dumping them down a mine shaft in a national heritage-listed area — and ordered to clean it up.

item.title: Former officers who blew the whistle on NSW Police's toxic culture react to report findings
item.description: Former NSW Police officers who blew the whistle on the toxic culture of the force have welcomed an independent report "validating" their experiences.

item.title: Pastoralists reunite for friendly competition at outback gymkhana and motorkhana after welcome rain
item.description: The trip to Marree in South Australia looked a little different for competitors in this year's annual gymkhana and motorkhana, with the outback blooming after record-breaking rain.

item.title: Angus Taylor brushes away questions on One Nation preference deal — as it happened
item.description: The opposition leader has brushed away questions on a preference deal with One Nation at the next election.

item.title: ASX ends week on a high as investors await SpaceX share launch — as it happened
item.description: The ASX end the week on a high amid hopes of a Middle East peace deal, while SpaceX's IPO prepares for lift-off on the Nasdaq. See how the trading day unfolded on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Soaring thefts force WA mandarin grower to close roadside stall after 17 years
item.description: Mandarin grower Bruce Crain says people's growing dishonesty, including the theft of 70 bags in one weekend, makes it impossible to offer a roadside service.

item.title: Closure of Mackay private hospital a loss that will 'make everything worse'
item.description: The 34-bed Mackay Private Hospital is closing its doors with the operator citing "rising operational and maintenance" costs. People who have been treated there say it is a huge loss for physical and mental rehabilitation care in the north Queensland.

item.title: Human remains found eight years after Sydney woman Nadire Sensoy's alleged murder
item.description: Nadire Sensoy disappeared from Sydney's west in 2018, with her disappearance triggering a police reward and coronial inquest.

item.title: World Cup: When do the Socceroos play and how to watch Australia vs Türkiye
item.description: The Socceroos will face Türkiye in their first Group D match at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Here's everything you need to know.

item.title: The Australian connection behind Roxane Gay and Channing Tatum's upcoming novel
item.description: It's possible the ball may not have got rolling on Roxane Gay and Channing Tatum's upcoming novel if it wasn't for an Australian connection. Gay will appear at Vivid Sydney in a wide-ranging discussion.

item.title: Cartier exhibition opens at Melbourne's NGV with hundreds of items never seen in Australia
item.description: Glittering tiaras and dazzling necklaces worn by Hollywood icons such as Elizabeth Taylor and Grace Kelly are featured in an exhibition at the National Gallery of Victoria in Melbourne.

item.title: Creditors of Nathan River Resources criticise NT government for lack of oversight of mining company
item.description: In the wake of Nathan River Resources entering into voluntary administration, creditors have criticised the NT government for its lack of oversight of the struggling company. ASIC documents show the mining company owes more than $360 million.

item.title: Big Splash water park will go up for sale with slides intact, owners say
item.description: The site will not undergo redevelopment or demolition before its sale and locals say it is the result they fought for.

item.title: Johnny Warren inspiring Socceroos coach as he prepares to make history at FIFA World Cup
item.description: Australian soccer legend Johnny Warren once asked why Australia was content with just qualifying for a World Cup. Australia coach Tony Popovic is remembering that as he aims higher than ever before at the tournament.

item.title: Government agencies told to police own AI use missed first transparency test
item.description: The ABC can reveal dozens of federal agencies missed mandatory deadlines to disclose how they are currently using AI.

item.title: NT government cancels controversial $1.1 million private midwife contract with Claire Marks
item.description: The Northern Territory government has axed a $1.1 million contract awarded to a Darwin midwife, banned from working unsupervised, almost 10 months after doctors began raising concerns about the deal.

item.title: Childcare paedophile Ashley Paul Griffith loses appeal to reduce non-parole period
item.description: The former childcare worker sexually abused dozens of children at centres for almost two decades.

item.title: What a career in forensics looks like while solving crime in the outback
item.description: From fingerprinting in the lab to photographing cattle from a helicopter, this forensic officer covers a beat the size of Victoria.

item.title: How Mexico World Cup goal scorer Raúl Jiménez came back from horrific head injury
item.description: In 2020, Mexico World Cup goal scorer Raúl Jiménez was lying on the turf of the Arsenal Stadium receiving oxygen after fracturing his skull and injuring his brain.

item.title: Mid-season nightmare as Camden football club loses clubhouse to fire
item.description: An 800-member club in Sydney's Macarthur region scrambles to find alternative fields for 30 games a week after losing its clubhouse to fire.

item.title: Roxane Gay on politics and AI
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Firefighters union and secretary trying to block IBAC's Operation Richmond report
item.description: The United Firefighters Union and its secretary Peter Marshall are behind a legal attempt to stop a report by Victoria's anti-corruption watchdog from being published.

item.title: Shakira, Andrea Bocelli and Salma Hayek kickstart World Cup at opening ceremony
item.description: The World Cup 2026 opens with a vibrant ceremony as Shakira, Andrea Bocelli and a cavalcade of Latino stars blend with Indigenous Mexican displays at the Estadio Azteca.

item.title: Pig Feast explores Indigenous land rights in West Papua but key leader Mama Yasinta changed her tune
item.description: A key Indigenous leader in West Papua appeared in a documentary criticising an Indonesian government program, but then changed sides and disappeared. Her family suspects foul play.

item.title: Thailand's Princess Bajrakitiyabha dies aged 47 after long illness, royal palace says
item.description: Princess Bajrakitiyabha Narendira Debyavati, who had spent the past three and a half years in a coma due to a heart condition, is being remembered for her efforts to improve the livelihoods of female prisoners and her diplomatic career.

item.title: What are the rules around using fire pits at home?
item.description: There are rules when it comes to using fire pits and things to be cautious about, including open-air fires being a nuisance to your neighbours.

item.title: Gus Lamont's grandparent fined over gun offence unrelated to 4yo boy's disappearance
item.description: The grandparent of missing four-year-old Gus Lamont has been fined $10,500 for possession of a gun silencer at the family's Oak Park Station, in South Australia's far north. Police have previously said the offence is not related to his disappearance.

item.title: Tony Popovic on playing and coaching in World Cups
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: NT government says land-clearing dispute with Claravale Station has reached settlement, a year after first announcing agreement
item.description: For years, the Northern Territory government has been locked in a legal battle with Claravale Station over allegations its owners cleared hundreds of hectares without a permit. But it is not the first time the government has announced the settlement deal.

item.title: VCE students, parents 'shocked' as Chinese language school enters administration
item.description: Dozens of students completing VCE Chinese language subjects face an uncertain future after a Chinese community language school goes into administration.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Opening ceremony delights, hydration break drama and Haiti's jersey ban
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is up and running, launched by a colourful opening ceremony and comprehensive win for Mexico. Here are the quick hits from the opening day of the tournament.

item.title: NDIS labour hire provider United Foundation enters administration citing registration changes and rising costs
item.description: NDIS labour hire provider United Foundation has entered voluntary administration blaming regulatory changes, delayed payments and rising costs.

item.title: Liberty Bell Bay workers given until Monday to find out if smelter will secure alternative funding
item.description: Workers at Tasmania's troubled Liberty Bell Bay smelter have been told administrators have until Monday to secure alternative funding, after a member of the preferred consortium bidding to take over the manganese smelter effectively pulled out.

item.title: Inquiry examining NDIS spending cuts told 240,000 people would exit the scheme
item.description: More than 240,000 people would exit the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) over four years according to modelling as part of a plan to cut spending on the multi-billion-dollar program.

item.title: Federal government has contracts worth more than $650m with embattled KPMG
item.description: Parliamentary library data reveals the federal government has almost 300 active contracts with accounting firm KPMG, which is being investigated over claims it misused confidential client information.

item.title: Victim's mother gives powerful statement to court ahead of Sydney childcare worker's sentencing
item.description: A mother whose son was abused by a Sydney out-of-school-hours care worker has spoken of the daily torment she now faces.

item.title: Queensland prosecutors seek to retry man acquitted of rape in double jeopardy laws test
item.description: It comes in the wake of Queensland's DNA testing debacle, which triggered the retesting of forensic samples going back years.

item.title: One Nation denies Melbourne fundraiser was moved due to protest concerns
item.description: A One Nation fundraiser in Melbourne has been moved after several protest groups vowed to demonstrate outside, according to police.

item.title: Gavin Preston's murderer Jaeden Tito had 'kind, caring nature' his mother tells court using ChatGPT
item.description: The mother of a man found guilty of murdering underworld figure Gavin Preston has told the Supreme Court her son has always been polite and considerate.

item.title: Fashion designer Katharina Mildren embraces social media to build thriving brand
item.description: Katharina Mildren's clothes have attracted legions of fans online and she ships items around the world, but there is one place you will not find her garments: in a shop.

item.title: Medical care Mark Bombara received before Floreat murders to be examined by inquest
item.description: The main focus of a coronial inquest into the death of a Perth mother and her daughter will be the medical care their killer Mark Bombara received in the lead up to the double-murder suicide, a coroner reveals.

item.title: Socceroos coach Tony Popovic signs contract extension to stay on until 2027 Asian Cup
item.description: Socceroos coach Tony Popovic gets his contract extended just days before Australia's World Cup kicks off to take the national team through to next year's Asian Cup.

item.title: Court hears of daughters' grief over fatal crash as Canberra man faces sentencing for negligent driving
item.description: The daughters of a motorcyclist killed in Canberra's north tried to reach him for their nightly phone call as he lay dying on the road, an ACT court has heard.

item.title: Australian sprinter Jemma Stapleton mourned after death in Thailand, two weeks after birthday
item.description: The brother of Stawell Gift finalist Jemma Stapleton remembers her "bright smile" and expresses gratitude for the outpouring of grief and support.

item.title: Andrew Forrest will not review Fortescue's traditional owner dealings after $150m compensation loss
item.description: Mining magnate Andrew "Twiggy" Forrest has defended his company's approach to Indigenous agreements after a court ordered the miner to pay $150 million in compensation to the Yindjibarndi people.

item.title: Pair who killed Greenbushes man Raymond Smith given lengthy jail sentences
item.description: A judge said the men, who were convicted of killing 68-year-old Raymond Smith during a drug-fuelled home invasion in the WA town of Greenbushes, showed no remorse.

item.title: Thousands of fans celebrate on the streets of Mexico City after opening World Cup win
item.description: Sport news, live scores, features, analysis and photos from the world of sport. Comprehensive coverage of AFL, cricket, football, rugby league and rugby union. Audio interviews. News stories and highlights in video. Sports news bulletin.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Mexicans produce all-night rager after opening win. Will the US and Canada follow suit?
item.description: Thousands of Mexican fans spill onto the streets of Mexico City after El Tri's opening World Cup triumph against South Africa.

item.title: Former Tasmanian school principal Gary Raymond Sykes faces 'inevitable' prison term for child sex abuse
item.description: A former school principal is staring down a prison term after robbing a "vulnerable" teenage girl of her innocence, a Tasmanian court has heard.

item.title: Mackay Sugar mills shut by cyber attack hope to reopen next week
item.description: Mackay Sugar tells growers it is aiming to restart cane processing early next week, after a cyber attack forced it to stop on Wednesday.

item.title: Byron Bluesfest may have traded while insolvent for months before collapse
item.description: The iconic Byron Bay music festival may have been trading while insolvent for months before it collapsed, according to a new liquidator's report.

item.title: Police reveal 11 cases assessed for possible links to Ivan Milat
item.description: A parliamentary inquiry has heard a police taskforce identified 11 unsolved homicide and missing persons cases with potential links to Ivan Milat.

item.title: Canberra Liberals leader Mark Parton takes aim at financial 'red hole' after ACT government releases budget
item.description: The Canberra Liberals and the ACT Greens issue their responses to the ACT budget, criticising the government's fiscal plan, principally around cost of living, transport and overall management of the territory's finances.

item.title: Queensland deputy premier says he has no sympathy for 'juvenile grubs' in youth detention lockdown
item.description: Jarrod Bleijie says children inside youth detention centres are largely to blame for lockdowns, and it's the staff that need protection.

item.title: Warm air mass over Australia during June
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Darwin bikie Brandt Graham hospitalised with injuries from police dog during re-capture
item.description: A Top End bikie who allegedly escaped police custody last week has been hospitalised after being bitten by a police dog during his re-capture.

item.title: Cats continue alarming Suns slide as Hardwick lashes 'ridiculous' rule
item.description: Geelong records a 45-point win over Gold Coast as Suns coach Damien Hardwick tees off at the officiating.

item.title: Mitre 10 franchisee battles to stop Bunnings opening next door in Jimboomba
item.description: A small town retailer is battling it out with Australia's biggest hardware giant in the Federal Court to stop Bunnings from opening up literally next door to his Mitre 10 franchise.

item.title: Friday Finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Resources Minister Madeleine King backs unions' right to strike at BHP's Port Hedland operations
item.description: Federal Resources Minister Madeleine King says workers threatening strike action at the nation's biggest port deserve "every single cent" of what they earn while a peak industry body says they are "letting down everyone".

item.title: Two dead as Taliban opens fire at protest after women arrested for dress code violations
item.description: The protest followed the arrests of dozens of women for alleged dress code violation in the Afghan city of Herat.

item.title: NT Police arrest alleged drug dealer who had samurai swords, batons, pills
item.description: Police charge a 45-year-old woman with a range of drug, weapons and other offences after a group of teenagers were caught stealing cat food to allegedly swap with her for cannabis.

item.title: Four dolphins found dead in Adelaide in recent days
item.description: Environmental authorities are investigating what caused the death of a Port River dolphin, believed to be Zoom, this week after several dolphin carcasses washed up on Adelaide beaches in recent days.

item.title: Dolphins make it six on the trot with Rooster thrashing
item.description: The Dolphins have continued their march towards a maiden NRL finals appearance with a 48-10 trouncing of the Sydney Roosters at Lang Park.

item.title: Giant of British art David Hockney dies aged 88 after decades inspired by southern California
item.description: British artist David Hockney, whose paintings of pools shimmering in the Los Angeles sunshine became icons of 20th-century art, dies aged 88.

item.title: Pierre Gasly reinstated to Monaco Grand Prix podium after Alpine succeeds with appeal
item.description: Alpine driver Pierre Gasly's third-place finish at the Monaco Grand Prix has been reinstated, with racing officials accepting an appeal filed by the Formula 1 team.

item.title: Meg Harris qualifies for 100m freestyle at Glasgow Commonwealth Games
item.description: After years as a relay specialist, Meg Harris qualifies for the 100m freestyle at the Commonwealth Games and Pan Pacific Swimming Championships.

item.title: Elon Musk becomes world's first trillionaire as SpaceX lists on US stock market
item.description: "SpaceX wants to be able to take you to the moon, take you to Mars, and ultimately beyond," Mr Musk said at a launch event in Starbase, Texas.

item.title: Both US and Iran say peace is close as mediator Pakistan says text of deal 'agreed'
item.description: Mediator Pakistan says the text of a peace deal between the US and Iran has been "agreed".

item.title: Live: Canadian joy as supersub saves World Cup party, Ghana star denied visa
item.description: World Cup co-host Canada gets its campaign underway in Toronto when it takes on Bosnia and Herzegovina. Follow live.

item.title: 'I wasn't expecting the drama': What you've learnt since owning a pet
item.description: Pets can be lifelong companions, but they can also test us in ways we never imagined. We asked you to share your stories. Here's what you told us.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Testing your knowledge of ABBA, the World Cup mascots and some fruity language
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

item.title: How Biladi and Blue Shield are helping to preserve the cultural heritage of war-torn countries
item.description: Non-governmental organisations Biladi and Blue Shield are among the design experts using new technologies to create detailed records of cultural heritage and help rebuild after conflict.

item.title: Elon Musk is the world's first trillionaire. Here's what that looks like
item.description: Elon Musk has become the world's first trillionaire. It is hard to fathom a figure so staggering, but let's give it a shot.

item.title: Abbott and Hanson's ancient feud is rife with political drama and biblical comeuppance
item.description: Tony Abbott has been back in the political cycle for barely a fortnight and already the air is redolent with garment-rending, ancient feuds, biblical comeuppance and a listening tour.

item.title: While Britain's defence strategy comes under fire, the nuclear arms race continues
item.description: The prospect of nuclear weapons being used in either Ukraine or the Middle East, rather than being fired between Moscow, Washington or Beijing has risen.

item.title: Wet conditions set to ease in parts of Queensland after spell of 'unseasonal' rain
item.description: Most Queenslanders are in for warmer and brighter weather this weekend, but scattered showers are expected to continue along parts of the coast.

item.title: How university degrees became a dating deal breaker
item.description: The ranks of Australia's so-called power couples, in which both partners are university-educated, have almost quadrupled over 20 years. This could have profound implications for social inequality.

item.title: Parts of Australia on track for warmest start to winter on record amid influx of tropical air
item.description: An influx of humid, tropical air has resulted in a remarkably warm and relatively wet start to winter across Australia.

item.title: Scientists share discoveries about Australia's polar past and the dinosaurs that roamed
item.description: Palaeontologists say evidence pointing to Australia's polar past shows that many dinosaurs roamed the country when the south east was still within the Antarctic Circle.

item.title: Just how far can the Socceroos go at the FIFA World Cup, and what is a pass mark?
item.description: This crop of Socceroos has been labelled the "next generation", but that doesn't mean they aren't aiming high at the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Your guide to every stadium at the 2026 World Cup
item.description: From volcano mimics to modern super-stadia with retractable roofs, the grounds of the 2026 FIFA World Cup are among the most spectacular on Earth. Here's your guide to each of the 16 stadiums across Canada, Mexico and the United States.

item.title: Agnes Water films 'time capsule' documentary to record life in 2026
item.description: Schoolchildren starring in a documentary about their quaint home town on the Queensland coast hope the publicity does not change what they love about it.

item.title: Australia's new ambassador gets to work in Washington ahead of 'robust' tariff negotiation
item.description: Greg Moriarty says he will "robustly" push back on the Trump administration's "extremely disappointing" new tariff plan and may recruit Australian businesses to help take up the fight.

item.title: Why we cannot name high-profile Queensland man at centre of Cairns extortion case
item.description: There is a highly secretive and unusual court case playing out behind closed doors in Far North Queensland involving a prominent married man and his alleged affair, a non-publication order we are not allowed to see and an allegation of extortion we cannot tell you much about.

item.title: The Interview: Reuben Kaye on the cost and the nourishment of being an artist in Australia
item.description: The new artistic director of the Adelaide Cabaret Festival shares what's unique about the art form in Australia and what his teenage self would think about who he is today.

item.title: Feral sambar deer likely brought to SA for hunting purposes, Invasive Species Council says
item.description: One of Australia's largest feral deer species was spotted in SA's Riverland for the first time earlier this month. While its existence is being investigated, the Invasive Species Council says it was likely transported across state lines for hunting purposes.

item.title: Australian kids returned from Syria deserve compassion, says grandfather
item.description: Jasmine was five years old and so traumatised that when she woke up trying to scream, she couldn't make a sound.
